title: Min-Max Scaling (Normalization)
concept: Min-Max scaling, or normalization, shifts and squashes numeric values into a strict 0 to 1 range.

exercises.prompt: Warmup: Why do distance-based algorithms like KNN require feature scaling?

options.text: To prevent features with large numeric ranges from dominating the distance calculations
To convert category names into numbers

exercises.explain: Right! Scaling keeps all features on a level playing field so no single feature dominates purely because of its units.

cards.title: Scores out of 100
cards.body: Whether a test was graded out of 40 points or 100 points, converting it to a percentage (0% to 100%) makes them instantly comparable. Normalization does the exact same thing for any feature.

cards.title: Min-Max Scaling
cards.body: Min-Max scaling (often called normalization) transforms numeric values so they fit strictly between 0 and 1. The formula is: (x - min) / (max - min).

cards.title: The volume slider
cards.body: Think of adjusting the volume on a speaker from 0 (mute) to 1 (max volume). No matter how loud the original sound wave was, the slider maps it to this standard range.

cards.title: Scaling Age
cards.body: If the youngest customer is 20 (min) and the oldest is 60 (max), a customer who is 40 (halfway) gets scaled to: (40 - 20) / (60 - 20) = 0.5. A customer who is 60 maps to 1.0.

cards.title: Strict bounds
cards.body: What if new data is outside the original min-max range? If a customer aged 70 signs up, their scaled value is 1.25, exceeding 1. We must handle new extremes carefully!

cards.title: How Min-Max works
cards.body: Squashing any range of values to fit exactly between 0 and 1:

exercises.prompt: What are the default minimum and maximum values after applying standard Min-Max scaling?
exercises.explain: Min-Max scaling by default squashes all numbers into a strict range between 0 and 1.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Under Min-Max scaling, the absolute minimum value in the training column will always map to exactly 0.
exercises.explain: True! In the formula (x - min) / (max - min), when x is the minimum value, the numerator becomes 0, so the result is 0.

exercises.sentence: The formula for Min-Max scaling is (x - min) divided by ___.
exercises.explain: Dividing by the range (max - min) scales the values so that the maximum value maps to exactly 1.

exercises.prompt: If the minimum value in a dataset is 10 and the maximum is 50, what does the value 30 scale to under Min-Max scaling?
exercises.explain: Using the formula: (30 - 10) / (50 - 10) = 20 / 40 = 0.5, which is exactly halfway.

exercises.prompt: You are building a neural network to process grayscale images where pixel brightness values range from 0 to 255. You decide to normalize the pixels. If a pixel has a raw value of 127.5, what will its normalized value be, and why?

options.text: 0.5, because 127.5 is exactly halfway between the minimum (0) and maximum (255) pixel values
0.0, because the minimum pixel value is subtracted
127.5, because normalization does not affect pixel values

exercises.explain: Correct! (127.5 - 0) / (255 - 0) = 127.5 / 255 = 0.5. Normalizing pixel values to a 0-1 range is a standard preprocessing step in image models. Fantastic job mastering Min-Max scaling!
